title: platform-user — inbound + outbound webhooks
description: This module publishes NO webhooks. Rationale: platform-user manages Academorix internal staff — there is no external integration party that consumes lifecycle events (an HRIS would be the consumer, but Academorix does not run one at this tier; if we adopt one in the future, an outbound `platform_user.hired` / `.offboarded` webhook would join this list). Inbound webhooks are similarly absent — there is no external identity provider posting into the platform-user surface (that's the future `identity/sso` module's job, Wave 1c). PagerDuty integration is one-way outbound API calls, not webhook-shaped. This file exists for uniform module shape.

outbound.description: No outbound webhooks. Emissions to Slack (via internal-notifications transport) + PagerDuty (via on-call transport) do NOT go through the customer-webhook pipeline — those are internal integrations owned by their respective transports.

future: If Academorix adopts an HRIS (BambooHR, Rippling, Deel, etc.), an outbound webhook contract lands here: `platform_user.hired`, `platform_user.department_changed`, `platform_user.offboarded` with the standard HMAC-signed envelope. Consumer would be the HRIS's inbound integration.

If Academorix integrates a directory-sync provider (Okta SCIM, Azure AD Graph), inbound webhooks land here to signal upstream identity mutations (rename, department reassignment) that this module should propagate to the local platform_users row.
